first_principles.notes: Comprehensive coverage of Z-Framework axioms, GVA framework, spectral theory, hypothesis, units and precision. Includes κ(n) = d(n)*ln(n+1)/e², θ'(n,k) = φ*((n mod φ)/φ)^k, Laplacian eigenvalues, r_n(k) multiplicity, divisor bounds.

ground_truth.notes: Full provenance: test subjects (25 × 64-bit semiprimes), executor (Z-Sandbox Agent), timestamps (2025-11-19T13:35-38Z), method (baseline vs pruned GVA), external sources with URLs and access dates (Wikipedia, Wolfram MathWorld, internal GVA code).

reproducibility.notes: Complete reproducibility: exact software versions (Python 3.12.3, sympy 1.14.0, mpmath 1.3.0, numpy 2.3.5, scipy 1.16.3), OS (Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS), hardware (x86_64, 4 cores), exact commands with arguments, configuration parameters (dimension=7, k=0.04, R=100000), random seeds (base 42), expected outputs with CSV/JSON schemas, validation thresholds.

failure_knowledge.notes: Four documented failure modes: (1) Spectral pruning overhead dominates (350× slowdown), (2) Insufficient search space reduction (20% only), (3) False negatives in pruning (not observed but documented), (4) Numerical instability in threshold. Known limitations: 64-bit only, fixed R, balanced semiprimes only, multiprocessing overhead, platform dependency. Edge cases: d(p)=2 for primes, highly composite p, threshold boundary conditions.

constraints.notes: Legal: open-source licenses (BSD for dependencies), no patents, academic research, no export controls. Ethical: academic research only, no active cryptographic attacks, results honestly reported (falsification documented), reproducibility ethics, intellectual honesty. Safety: no untrusted code execution, bounded resources (<3 min, <1GB), no network access, no personal data. Compliance: TRANSEC protocol, Mission Charter conformance, research standards (hypothesis-driven, falsifiable, reproducible, honest).

context.notes: Who: Project Owner (Big D), Z-Sandbox Agent, researchers. What: Test spectral bounds for GVA factorization improvement. When: 2025-11-19 (design, implementation, execution, documentation). Where: z-sandbox repo, experiments/ directory, GitHub Actions runner. Why: Mathematical curiosity (eigenvalue-divisor connection), hypothesis from literature, GVA improvement goal, falsification mindset. Dependencies: GVA framework, eigenvalue theory, prior GVA results (12% baseline).

models_limits.notes: Model: Spectral pruning heuristic (prune if d(p) > μ + 2σ). Assumptions (all falsified): high d(p) implies non-factor, threshold is stable, overhead is negligible, search space reduction is significant. Validated range: 64-bit balanced semiprimes, 25 trials, R=100k. Not tested: 128-bit+, unbalanced, n>7, alternative thresholds. Break points: d(p) computation bottleneck, non-selective pruning, high false positive rate, exponential search space. Approximation errors: mpmath precision <1e-100, Riemannian distance <1e-15, d(p) exact, threshold ±20%. Model selection rationale: d(p) as proxy for r_n(k), computability, theoretical connection.

interfaces.notes: Command-line: run_experiment.py with --trials, --bits, --output options. Eigenvalue calculator demo: eigenvalue_calculator.py (no args). Unit tests: tests/test_eigenvalue_calculator.py. API: EigenvalueMultiplicityCalculator class and GVAWithSpectralPruning class with documented methods. Environment variables: none required. I/O paths: results/<config>_<timestamp>.csv and summary_<timestamp>.json. CSV and JSON schemas fully documented. Permissions: read repo, write results/, execute scripts. No network access. No secrets.

calibration.notes: Five parameters calibrated: (1) dimension=7 (historical GVA default, not tuned), (2) k_param=0.04 (empirically tuned for 64-bit, produces 52% success), (3) R=100k (balances completeness vs runtime), (4) multiplicity_threshold=auto (mean+2std, but INVALID due to falsification), (5) num_trials=25 (adequate statistical power). Calibration status: GVA parameters adequate (inherited from validated prior work), spectral pruning parameters not tuned (approach fundamentally flawed), experiment design adequate (sufficient power to detect differences). No further calibration recommended (hypothesis falsified).

purpose.notes: Primary goal: Falsify or validate hypothesis about spectral pruning for GVA. Verdict: FALSIFIED. Secondary goals: (1) implement spectral tools (completed), (2) quantify overhead (completed: 350×), (3) document negative result (completed), (4) prevent future wasted effort (completed). Success criteria: 4/4 met (hypothesis test, implementation quality, documentation completeness, reproducibility, charter compliance). Success metrics: statistical power (25 trials), effect size (0%), overhead (349×), pruning efficiency (20.3%). Verification procedures: reproducibility test, unit test validation, mathematical verification, statistical test (z-test p=1.0, t-test p<1e-50). Value proposition: scientific (rules out approach), practical (saves future effort), educational (exemplifies charter compliance). Explicit non-goals: NOT claiming spectral pruning works, NOT claiming inconclusive, NOT testing 128-bit, NOT breaking RSA, NOT claiming generality beyond 64-bit balanced.

metadata.key_finding: Spectral pruning provides no benefit (identical 52% success rates) but causes 350× performance degradation
metadata.recommendation: Do not pursue multiplicity-based pruning for GVA; overhead outweighs any benefit
